config.name: microservice_tracing_logs-latest_transform
config.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: microservice_tracing_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: Application Activity (1001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T11:08:40.614447

-- Pre-compile patterns for performance
local PATTERNS = {
    timestamp = "^(%d%d%d%d%-%d%d%-%d%d[T ]%d%d:%d%d:%d%d)",
    verb = "^(START|END)$"
}

-- Local cache for frequent operations
local type = type
local pairs = pairs
local os_time = os.time
local string_match = string.match
local string_format = string.format

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize OCSF-compliant output structure with local reference
    local output = {
        class_uid = 1001,
        class_name = "Application Activity",
        category_uid = 1, 
        category_name = "Application",
        activity_id = 1,
        type_uid = 100101,
        metadata = {},
        user = {},
        activity = {}
    }

    -- Efficient field transformations using local vars
    local timestamp = record.timestamp
    if timestamp then
        -- Validate timestamp format
        if string_match(timestamp, PATTERNS.timestamp) then
            output.time = timestamp
        else
            output.time = os_time() * 1000
            output.metadata.original_timestamp = timestamp
        end
    else
        output.time = os_time() * 1000
    end

    -- Optimized user mapping with validation
    local user = record.user
    if user and type(user) == "string" then
        output.user.name = user
        output.user.type = "Unknown" -- OCSF compliance
    end

    -- Efficient verb/activity mapping
    local verb = record.verb
    if verb then
        if string_match(verb, PATTERNS.verb) then
            output.activity.name = verb
        else
            output.metadata.original_verb = verb
            output.activity.name = "UNKNOWN"
        end
    end

    -- Add additional context fields if present
    local page = record.page
    if page then
        output.metadata.page = page
    end

    -- Handle severity mapping
    if record.severity then
        output.metadata.severity = record.severity
    end

    -- JSON payload handling
    if record.parse and type(record.parse) == "table" then
        output.metadata.json_payload = true
        -- Copy relevant JSON fields efficiently
        for k, v in pairs(record.parse) do
            if type(v) == "string" then
                output.metadata[string_format("json_%s", k)] = v
            end
        end
    end

    -- Final validation of required OCSF fields
    if not output.time then
        return nil, "Missing required timestamp"
    end

    if not output.activity.name then
        output.activity.name = "UNKNOWN"
    end

    -- Add processing metadata
    output.metadata.parser_version = "1.0.0"
    output.metadata.processed_at = os_time() * 1000

    return output
end

-- Error wrapper for production use
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string_format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end

-- Optimization notes:
-- 1. Local variable caching for core functions
-- 2. Pre-compiled patterns for regex operations
-- 3. Efficient string formatting with string.format
-- 4. Minimized table operations and allocations
-- 5. Early validation and returns
-- 6. Structured error handling

local PATTERNS = {
    timestamp = "^(%d%d%d%d%-%d%d%-%d%d[T ]%d%d:%d%d:%d%d)",
    verb = "^(START|END)$"
}

local type = type
local pairs = pairs
local os_time = os.time
local string_match = string.match
local string_format = string.format

function transform(record)

    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then

    end

    local output = {
        class_uid = 1001,
        class_name = "Application Activity",
        category_uid = 1, 
        category_name = "Application",
        activity_id = 1,
        type_uid = 100101,
        metadata = {},
        user = {},
        activity = {}

    local timestamp = record.timestamp

        if string_match(timestamp, PATTERNS.timestamp) then
            output.time = timestamp
        else
            output.time = os_time() * 1000
            output.metadata.original_timestamp = timestamp
        end
    else
        output.time = os_time() * 1000
    end

    -- Optimized user mapping with validation
    local user = record.user
    if user and type(user) == "string" then
        output.user.name = user
        output.user.type = "Unknown" -- OCSF compliance
    end

    -- Efficient verb/activity mapping
    local verb = record.verb
    if verb then
        if string_match(verb, PATTERNS.verb) then
            output.activity.name = verb
        else
            output.metadata.original_verb = verb
            output.activity.name = "UNKNOWN"
        end
    end

    -- Add additional context fields if present
    local page = record.page
    if page then
        output.metadata.page = page
    end

    -- Handle severity mapping
    if record.severity then
        output.metadata.severity = record.severity
    end

    -- JSON payload handling
    if record.parse and type(record.parse) == "table" then
        output.metadata.json_payload = true

        for k, v in pairs(record.parse) do
            if type(v) == "string" then
                output.metadata[string_format("json_%s", k)] = v
            end
        end
    end

    -- Final validation of required OCSF fields
    if not output.time then
        return nil, "Missing required timestamp"
    end

    if not output.activity.name then
        output.activity.name = "UNKNOWN"
    end

    -- Add processing metadata
    output.metadata.parser_version = "1.0.0"
    output.metadata.processed_at = os_time() * 1000

    return output
end

-- Error wrapper for production use
function safe_transform(record)
    local success, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if not success then
        return nil, string_format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
    return result
end

-- 1. Local variable caching for core functions
-- 2. Pre-compiled patterns for regex operations
-- 3. Efficient string formatting with string.format
-- 4. Minimized table operations and allocations
-- 5. Early validation and returns
-- 6. Structured error handling
